description: Cross-surah narrative arcs mapping stories that span multiple surahs in the Quran

narratives.description: The creation of the first human, the angels' prostration, Iblis's refusal, the forbidden tree, and the descent to earth

surah_sequence.summary: Allah tells the angels He will place a khalifah on earth; teaches Adam the names; commands prostration — Iblis refuses
Adam and his wife placed in Paradise; warned about the forbidden tree; Shaytan causes them to slip
Adam receives words of repentance; Allah accepts; they are sent to earth with guidance
Detailed account of Iblis's refusal and his vow to mislead humanity from every direction
Shaytan whispers to Adam and his wife; they eat from the tree; their shame is exposed; they repent
Clothing of taqwa is best; warning not to let Shaytan deceive as he did your parents
Creation from clay, breathing of the spirit, Iblis's arrogance, his respite until the Day
Iblis refuses to prostrate to one created from clay; given respite; vows to mislead Adam's offspring
Iblis was of the jinn; he disobeyed — will you take him and his offspring as allies?
Adam forgot his covenant; Shaytan whispered; the tree of eternity; Adam disobeyed then was guided
Creation from clay; Iblis's arrogance; expelled; granted respite; swears to mislead all except the devoted
The story of Adam's two sons — Habil and Qabil; the first murder; the crow teaches burial

narratives.description: Ibrahim's search for truth, confrontation with idolatry, the fire miracle, sacrifice of his son, and building the Ka'bah

surah_sequence.summary: Ibrahim observes the stars, moon, and sun — rejects each as they set; declares devotion to the One Creator
Ibrahim argues with his people about idols; Allah raises his rank and grants him proof
Ibrahim confronts his father Azar about idol worship; warned of punishment; leaves his people
Ibrahim smashes the idols; challenged to prove it; thrown into the fire which Allah makes cool and safe
Ibrahim recounts his journey to truth; the One who created, guides, feeds, heals, causes death and gives life
Ibrahim calls his people; they attempt to burn him; Allah saves him; he emigrates
Ibrahim breaks the idols; his people build a furnace; he is thrown in but saved
Ibrahim prays for a righteous son; dreams of sacrificing him; both submit; Allah ransoms with a great sacrifice
Ibrahim's honored angel guests; glad tidings of a learned son; they reveal their mission to destroy Lut's people
Angels visit Ibrahim; Sarah laughs at news of Ishaq; Ibrahim pleads for Lut's people
Ibrahim tested and made a leader; with Ismail raises the foundations of the Ka'bah; prays for Mecca
Ibrahim's comprehensive dua: security for Mecca, distance from idols, prayer, forgiveness
Ibrahim shown the site of the Ka'bah; commanded to proclaim Hajj to mankind
Ibrahim's debates: the king who argued about giving life; Ibrahim asks Allah to show how He gives life to the dead
Ibrahim as a model — he disassociated from his people's idolatry except his prayer for his father

narratives.title_english: The Story of Prophet Musa (Moses) & Pharaoh
narratives.title_urdu: موسیٰ علیہ السلام اور فرعون کی کہانی
narratives.description: The most frequently told story in the Quran — Musa's birth, mission to Pharaoh, the Exodus, Mount Sinai, and the golden calf

surah_sequence.summary: Musa's birth; mother inspired to place him in the river; Pharaoh's wife adopts him; his mother nurses him
Musa cast into the river as a baby; raised in Pharaoh's household by Allah's decree
Musa reaches maturity; accidentally kills a man; flees Egypt when warned of a plot against him
Musa arrives in Madyan; helps two women water their flock; marries and stays for years
At the valley of Tuwa, Allah speaks from the burning bush; reveals the staff and glowing hand miracles; commands him to go to Pharaoh
Musa sees a fire; Allah speaks; grants signs; Musa requests Harun as support
Musa and Harun sent to Pharaoh; show signs; Pharaoh calls him a madman
Musa confronts Pharaoh with signs; contest with sorcerers; sorcerers believe; Pharaoh threatens them
Pharaoh calls Musa a sorcerer; the great gathering; sorcerers' staffs become snakes; Musa's staff swallows them; sorcerers prostrate in faith
Musa sent to Pharaoh; the sorcerers believe; Pharaoh's persecution; Musa tells his people to trust in Allah
The plagues sent upon Egypt: flood, locusts, lice, frogs, blood — Pharaoh's people refuse to believe
Allah commands Musa to travel by night with His servants; they reach the sea; Pharaoh pursues
Strike the sea with your staff — a dry path appears; the Israelites cross; Pharaoh follows
Pharaoh drowning declares belief; too late — his body preserved as a sign for future generations
After crossing, Israelites encounter idol worshippers; ask Musa for an idol; he refuses
Musa spends 40 nights on Mount Sinai; receives the Torah; asks to see Allah; the mountain crumbles
The golden calf worship in Musa's absence; his anger; throws the tablets; confronts Harun
Samiri makes the golden calf; people worship it; Musa returns furious; Samiri's punishment
The Israelites told to enter the land; they refuse; demand specific food; wandering in the desert
Musa tells his people to enter the Holy Land; they refuse — 40 years lost in the wilderness

narratives.description: The best of stories — Yusuf's journey from a prophetic dream through betrayal, slavery, prison, to power and family reunion in Egypt

surah_sequence.summary: Introduction — We relate to you the best of stories through this Quran
Young Yusuf tells his father about the dream of eleven stars, the sun, and the moon prostrating to him
Brothers plot out of jealousy; throw him in a well; bring false bloodied shirt to Ya'qub; 'beautiful patience'
A caravan finds and sells Yusuf; bought by the Egyptian minister; raised in his household
The minister's wife tries to seduce Yusuf; he resists; shirt torn from behind proves his innocence
Women of the city gossip; she invites them; they cut their hands upon seeing Yusuf's beauty; he prefers prison
In prison, Yusuf preaches tawheed; interprets dreams of two inmates — one freed, one executed
The king's dream of seven fat and seven lean cows; Yusuf interprets from prison; foretells famine
Yusuf's name cleared; appointed over Egypt's storehouses; begins his governance
Brothers come to Egypt for grain; Yusuf recognizes them; demands they bring their youngest brother
Binyamin comes; Yusuf hides a cup in his bag; brothers tested; Binyamin stays behind
Ya'qub's grief — complains only to Allah; 'Do not despair of Allah's mercy'
Brothers return desperate; Yusuf reveals himself; 'No blame upon you today'; sends shirt to cure Ya'qub's blindness
Ya'qub regains sight; family travels to Egypt; they prostrate — the dream fulfilled; Yusuf's grateful dua
Conclusion — lessons for people of understanding; the Quran is not fabricated but a confirmation

narratives.title_english: The Story of Isa & Maryam (Jesus & Mary)
narratives.title_urdu: عیسیٰ اور مریم علیہم السلام کی کہانی
narratives.description: The miraculous birth of Isa to the virgin Maryam, his prophetic mission, miracles, and ascension

surah_sequence.summary: Allah chose the family of Imran; Maryam's mother dedicates her; Zakariya appointed guardian; divine provisions
Zakariya prays for a son; granted Yahya; given the sign of three days without speech
Zakariya's prayer — old and grey; granted Yahya with wisdom and tenderness from childhood
Angels tell Maryam she is chosen and purified above all women of the worlds
Angels announce the Messiah, Isa son of Maryam — distinguished in this world and the Hereafter
Maryam withdraws; Jibril appears as a man; announces a pure son; she is amazed
Maryam conceives and gives birth alone under a palm tree; told to shake for dates and drink from the stream
Maryam brings baby to her people; they accuse her; infant Isa speaks from the cradle declaring he is Allah's servant
Isa's miracles — heals blind, cures leprosy, raises dead, creates bird from clay — all by Allah's permission
Allah recounts His favors to Isa; disciples ask for a table spread from heaven as a sign
Isa senses disbelief; asks who are his helpers for Allah; the disciples declare faith; the plotters plan
They did not kill or crucify Isa — it was made to appear so; Allah raised him to Himself
Allah tells Isa He will take and raise him; purify him from disbelievers; followers will prevail until the Day
Isa is a messenger and Word of Allah bestowed upon Maryam; do not say trinity — Allah is One
On the Day of Judgment — Allah asks Isa if he told people to worship him; Isa denies and submits
Isa is no more than a servant favored by Allah; made an example for the Children of Israel; a sign of the Hour

narratives.description: Nuh's 950-year mission, patient preaching, building the ark, the great flood, and the aftermath

surah_sequence.summary: Nuh calls his people day and night for centuries; they refuse, put fingers in ears, cover themselves
Nuh reports their stubbornness; asks for no disbeliever to remain; prays for forgiveness for believers
Nuh sent to his people; chiefs call him a madman; he persists; told to build the ark
Nuh warns his people; chiefs reject him as mere mortal with lowly followers; he argues patiently
Allah tells Nuh no more will believe; commanded to build the ark; mocked by passersby
The oven bursts; Nuh boards pairs of each species, family, and believers; the great flood; ark settles on Mount Judi
Nuh's son refuses to board — seeks refuge on a mountain; waves separate them; his son drowns
Nuh grieves for his son; Allah tells him his son was not of his family in faith; Nuh repents
Nuh's people called him a liar; he persists; they threaten stoning; Allah saves him and believers in the laden ship
Nuh remained among his people 950 years; the flood seized them while they were wrongdoers
Nuh's people denied; he called to his Lord; gates of heaven opened with rain; earth burst with springs

narratives.description: Young believers who fled persecution, slept in a cave for 309 years, and awakened to a changed world

surah_sequence.summary: Introduction — the companions of the cave and the inscription are among Allah's wondrous signs
They were youths who believed; fled from their people's shirk; took refuge in the cave seeking Allah's mercy
The sun passes their cave without disturbing them; they turn side to side; their dog stretches at the entrance
They awaken thinking they slept a day; one sent to the city for food; discovers the world has changed
Their discovery; people dispute their number — three, five, or seven; say my Lord knows best
They remained 309 years; Allah knows best the unseen of the heavens and earth

narratives.description: A righteous ruler who traveled east, west, and to the barrier against Gog and Magog

surah_sequence.summary: They ask about Dhul-Qarnayn; Allah established him; travels to the setting sun; punishes wrongdoers, rewards righteous
Journey to the rising of the sun; finds a people with no shelter
Between two mountains; people plagued by Ya'juj and Ma'juj; builds iron and copper barrier; 'this is a mercy from my Lord'
When Ya'juj and Ma'juj are let loose — they descend from every elevation; the true promise approaches

narratives.title_urdu: موسیٰ اور خضر علیہم السلام کی کہانی
narratives.description: Musa journeys to learn hidden wisdom from Khidr — three perplexing acts with divine reasons

surah_sequence.summary: Musa and his companion journey to the junction of two seas; forget their fish; find Khidr — a servant granted knowledge
Musa asks to follow; Khidr warns about patience; sets the condition — do not question until I explain
First test: Khidr damages a boat; Musa objects; Khidr reminds the condition
Second test: Khidr kills a youth; Musa is horrified; last warning
Third test: Khidr rebuilds a wall for free in unwelcoming town; Musa objects; they must part
Khidr reveals: boat — a tyrant king seizing ships; youth — would drive parents to disbelief; wall — hid treasure for orphans. 'I did not do it of my own accord'

narratives.description: Badr, Uhud, the Confederates, Hudaybiyya, and the Conquest of Mecca — the military and diplomatic turning points

surah_sequence.summary: Battle of Badr: believers set out; Allah promises help; angels descend; 'You did not throw when you threw — Allah threw'
Distribution of Badr spoils; Allah's planning vs. enemies; Shaytan deserts disbelievers
If 20 patient believers, they overcome 200; if 100, they overcome 1,000
Battle of Uhud: archers positioned; early success; archers leave post; tide turns
Uhud aftermath: wounds alternate; those who fled forgiven; lessons in perseverance
Archer disobedience; Allah tests hearts; Shaytan caused them to slip; Allah pardoned them
Martyrs of Uhud alive with their Lord; do not fear those who threaten
Battle of Ahzab: armies surround Medina; wind and unseen forces rout the enemy; the trench stands
Prophet as beautiful model during Ahzab; treachery of Banu Qurayza; siege and resolution
Treaty of Hudaybiyya declared a clear victory; pledge under the tree; Allah well-pleased
The pledge; Allah sent tranquility; restrained hands; the Hudaybiyya vision will be fulfilled
Vision confirmed — entering al-Masjid al-Haram safely; description of Prophet and companions
Conquest of Mecca: victory of Allah; people enter Islam in multitudes; glorify and seek forgiveness
Battle of Hunayn: initial confidence in numbers fails; Allah sends tranquility; the tide turns

narratives.description: The recurring pattern of prophetic warning, rejection, and divine punishment upon 'Ad, Thamud, Lut's people, Madyan, and others

surah_sequence.summary: 'Ad — Prophet Hud warns against idolatry; they demand punishment; devastating wind destroys them
'Ad — Hud calls to worship Allah alone; they are seized by the blast
'Ad — Hud warns the people who build monuments; they reject; destroyed
'Ad in al-Ahqaf; they see the cloud of punishment approaching; everything destroyed except dwellings
'Ad destroyed by furious screaming wind; seven nights and eight days; prostrate like hollow palm trunks
Thamud — Salih and the she-camel; they hamstring her; earthquake seizes them
Thamud — Salih tells them to seek forgiveness; they hamstring she-camel; three days then the blast
Thamud warned through she-camel; hamstrung; punishment overtook them
Thamud denied; the most wicked rose up; hamstrung her; Allah destroyed them
People of Lut — unprecedented sin; rained with stones
People of Lut — angels visit; cities overturned at dawn; stones of baked clay
People of Lut — reject Lut; rained with evil rain
People of Lut — angels come; dawn punishment; cities overturned; marked stones rain
Madyan — Shu'ayb warns against cheating; earthquake seizes them
Madyan — Shu'ayb calls for just weights; blast from sky destroys them
Summary: We destroyed 'Ad, Thamud, Qarun, Pharaoh — each seized for their sin

narratives.description: Sulayman's command over jinn, wind, and animals; the hoopoe; the Queen of Sheba; his death

surah_sequence.summary: Sulayman inherits Dawud; taught language of birds; armies of jinn, men, and birds; the ant's warning
The hoopoe absent; returns with news of Sheba — a queen with a great throne who worships the sun
Sulayman sends a letter beginning with Bismillah; invites the Queen to Islam
Queen sends gift; Sulayman refuses; throne transported instantly; she enters palace of glass; submits to Allah
Wind subservient; copper fountain; jinn work; when he died, only a worm eating his staff revealed it
Sulayman's test with horses; prayer for unmatched kingdom; wind and shayatin subjugated
Dawud and Sulayman judging the field; understanding given to Sulayman; shayatin dive for him
Sulayman did not disbelieve — but the shayatin did; magic taught; Harut and Marut

narratives.description: The arc of Pharaoh's tyranny — from oppression through the plagues to his drowning and body preservation

surah_sequence.summary: Pharaoh exalted himself; divided people; slaughtered sons; Allah wills to favor the oppressed
Musa sent with signs; a believing man from Pharaoh's family speaks up secretly
Pharaoh tells Haman to build a tower to reach heavens — his arrogance
Pharaoh boasts about the kingdom of Egypt; belittles Musa
Plagues: drought, flood, locusts, lice, frogs, blood — each time they promise then break it
Musa sent to Pharaoh; he proclaims 'I am your most exalted lord'; Allah seizes him
Musa told to march by night; Pharaoh pursues; they left gardens, springs, noble positions
The exodus; Pharaoh mobilizes army; sea struck; Israelites cross; Pharaoh follows
Pharaoh drowning declares belief — too late; body preserved as a sign
Pharaoh and Haman's fate — arrogant; seized and thrown into the sea; leaders calling to the Fire
Pharaoh's wife Asiya believed; prayed for home in Paradise near Allah — a model for believers

narratives.description: The trumpet, resurrection, reckoning, the scales, and the final destinations of Paradise and Hellfire

surah_sequence.summary: Cosmic upheaval: sun folded, stars scattered, mountains moved, seas set on fire; every soul will know what it brought
Sky breaks apart, stars scatter, oceans erupt, graves overturned
Earth shakes; brings forth burdens; people proceed in groups; atom's weight of good or evil seen
Trumpet blown — all fall unconscious; blown again — they stand looking
Trumpet blown; from graves they rush; 'Who raised us?'; 'This is what the Most Merciful promised'
Trumpet — Day of warning; every soul comes with a driver and a witness
Day of Sorting — trumpet; multitudes arrive; sky opened as gates; mountains moved as mirage
Single blast; earth and mountains crushed; heaven split; angels at edges; eight carry the Throne
At death, barrier until resurrection; trumpet; lineage won't help; scales weighed
Scales of justice — no soul wronged even mustard seed weight
The Striking Calamity — people like scattered moths; mountains like wool; heavy scales: pleasant life; light: the Abyss
Three groups: companions of right, left, and the forerunners nearest to Allah
Forerunners in Gardens of Pleasure — adorned couches; fruits, meat; companions of pure beauty
Companions of left — scorching fire; boiling water; shade of black smoke
Those who feared their Lord — two gardens; flowing springs; fruits in pairs; silk; 'Which favors would you deny?'
Paradise: gardens, silk, silver vessels, ginger drink, green garments; 'This is your reward'
Disbelievers driven to Hell in groups; believers to Paradise; gates opened; 'Peace be upon you'; angels glorifying

narratives.description: From shepherd to king — Dawud defeats Goliath, receives the Psalms, mountains and birds glorify with him

surah_sequence.summary: Israelites ask for a king; Talut appointed; Dawud kills Jalut; given the kingdom and wisdom
Dawud and Sulayman judge the field case; mountains and birds glorify with Dawud; taught to make armor
Mountains and birds commanded to glorify with Dawud; iron made soft; coats of mail
Dawud given strength; tested by two disputants; realizes and repents; made khalifah — judge with truth

narratives.description: The ultimate test of patience — Ayyub loses health and family yet never loses faith

surah_sequence.summary: Ayyub calls: 'Adversity has touched me, and You are the Most Merciful'; Allah removes affliction, restores family doubled
Ayyub calls to his Lord; told to strike ground — cool spring gushes; health and family restored

narratives.description: The prophet who departed in anger, was swallowed by a whale, repented in darkness, and was restored

surah_sequence.summary: Yunus flees; boarded ship; cast lots; swallowed by whale; in darkness glorifies Allah
Dhun-Nun's supplication: 'La ilaha illa Anta, Subhanaka'; Allah saves him from distress
Cast ashore weak; Allah grows gourd tree; sent to 100,000+; his people believe and are spared
Yunus's people — the only nation saved from punishment after initially rejecting their prophet
Be patient — do not be like the companion of the whale; had Allah's favor not reached him, he'd have been blamed

narratives.title_urdu: لقمان کی اپنے بیٹے کو نصیحت
narratives.description: Timeless parental wisdom — tawheed, gratitude, parents, prayer, character, and humility

surah_sequence.summary: Allah gave Luqman wisdom: 'Be grateful to Allah — whoever is grateful is grateful for himself'
First advice: Do not associate anything with Allah — shirk is tremendous injustice
Honor parents — mother carried in weakness; but if they urge shirk, do not obey yet keep kind company
Allah's omniscience — even a mustard seed hidden in a rock, Allah will bring it forth
Establish prayer, enjoin good, forbid evil, be patient; don't walk arrogantly; be moderate; lower your voice

narratives.description: Lut warns his people; angels visit; cities overturned at dawn with stones of baked clay

surah_sequence.summary: Lut warns; they approach men; they say expel him; rained with stones
Lut warns; they threaten; Allah saves his family except wife; rained evil rain
Angels come; people rush to his house; told to leave by night; cities overturned; stones of baked clay
Angels visit; people come with desire; dawn — cities turned upside down; marked stones rain
Lut tells people of unprecedented evil; they say bring punishment; angels tell Ibrahim
Lut's people denied warnings; sandstorm sent; Lut's family saved at dawn

narratives.description: The prophet of fair trade — sent to Madyan to end economic corruption

surah_sequence.summary: Shu'ayb sent to Madyan — worship Allah, give full measure; earthquake seizes them
Shu'ayb warns against shortchanging; they threaten stoning; blast from sky destroys them
Companions of the Thicket denied Shu'ayb; punishment of the Day of Shadow
To Madyan their brother Shu'ayb; earthquake seized them
